sql_array: CREATE TABLE items (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100), description TEXT);
INSERT INTO items (id, name, description) VALUES (1, 'Item1', 'This is the first Item'), (2, 'Item2', 'Contains special character like @'), (3, 'Item3', NULL);
CREATE TABLE xml_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, content XML);
SELECT id, strstr(name, 'item') AS found_index FROM items WHERE found_index IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, xpath_string('//item', content) AS xpath_result FROM xml_data WHERE xpath_string('//item', content) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS item_count FROM items GROUP BY strstr(name, 'Item') HAVING item_count > 1;
SELECT DISTINCT a.id FROM items a JOIN xml_data b ON strstr(a.name, b.content) IS NOT NULL;

WITH RECURSIVE num_series AS (SELECT 0 AS num UNION ALL SELECT num + 1 FROM num_series WHERE num < 10) SELECT num, strstr(name, CAST(num AS VARCHAR)) AS index_found FROM items WHERE index_found IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY strstr(name, 'Item')) AS item_partition_count FROM items ORDER BY item_partition_count DESC;
SELECT id, coalesce(strstr(name, 'Item'), -1) AS index_found FROM items WHERE description IS NULL OR description = '';
SELECT id FROM items WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM xml_data WHERE xpath_string('//item', content) IS NOT NULL AND strstr('XX', xpath_string('//item', content)) IS NOT NULL);
